name: Rihanna
description:
'''Robyn Rihanna Fenty''' ({{IPAc-en|r|i|ˈ|æ|n|ə|audio=En-us-rihanna.ogg}} {{Respell|ree|AN|ə}};{{refn|It is also common for English-language speakers to pronounce her name {{IPAc-en|r|i|'|a:|n|ə}} {{Respell|ree|AH|nə}}.<ref name="d722">{{cite web | last=Goldstein | first=Joelle | title=Rihanna Fans Freak Out After Learning They May Have Pronounced Her Name Wrong for Years | publisher=[[Yahoo! Entertainment]] | date=May 30, 2019 | url=https://www.yahoo.com/entertainment/rihanna-fans-freak-learning-may-231820987.html | access-date=October 14, 2025}}</ref>|group=n}} born February 20, 1988) is a Barbadian singer, businesswoman, and actress. An influential figure in popular culture, she is known for her [[multiple careers|multifaceted career]], artistic reinventions, and eclectic fashion style. Rihanna is one of the [[best-selling music artists of all time]], with estimated sales of over 250 million records.

After signing with [[Def Jam Recordings]], Rihanna debuted with the [[List of Caribbean music genres|Caribbean]]-inspired records ''[[Music of the Sun]]'' (2005) and ''[[A Girl Like Me (Rihanna album)|A Girl Like Me]]'' (2006), both of which reached the top ten of the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]]. Their respective lead singles, "[[Pon de Replay]]" and "[[SOS (Rihanna song)|SOS]]", peaked at numbers two and one on the US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]]. Adopting a more mature image, Rihanna rose to stardom and transitioned to [[dance-pop]] and [[contemporary R&B|R&B]] with her best-selling album ''[[Good Girl Gone Bad]]'' (2007) and its reissue, subtitled [[Good Girl Gone Bad: Reloaded|''Reloaded'']] (2008). The project yielded a string of successful songs, including the US number-one singles "[[Umbrella (song)|Umbrella]]", "[[Take a Bow (Rihanna song)|Take a Bow]]", and "[[Disturbia (song)|Disturbia]]". Her guest appearances on "[[Live Your Life (T.I. song)|Live Your Life]]", "[[Love the Way You Lie]]", and "[[The Monster (song)|The Monster]]" also topped the ''Billboard'' Hot 100.

After exploring more personal themes on the [[rock music|rock]]-influenced record ''[[Rated R (Rihanna album)|Rated R]]'' (2009), Rihanna returned to her more upbeat sound with the dance-pop albums [[Loud (Rihanna album)|''Loud'']] (2010) and ''[[Talk That Talk]]'' (2011), and topped the ''Billboard'' 200 with her [[synth-pop]] set ''[[Unapologetic]]'' (2012). Departing from Def Jam in favour of [[Roc Nation]], she released the eclectic ''[[Anti (album)|Anti]]'' (2016)—her second number-one album. These albums contained the US number-one singles "[[Rude Boy (Rihanna song)|Rude Boy]]", "[[Only Girl (In the World)]]", "[[What's My Name? (Rihanna song)|What's My Name?]]", "[[S&M (song)|S&M]]", "[[We Found Love]]", "[[Diamonds (Rihanna song)|Diamonds]]", and "[[Work (Rihanna song)|Work]]". Since 2016, Rihanna has been releasing singles and performing sporadically. She has also ventured into acting, and [[Rihanna videography#Film|her film roles]] include ''[[Battleship (film)|Battleship]]'' (2012), ''[[Home (2015 film)|Home]]'' (2015), ''[[Ocean's 8]]'' (2018), and ''[[Smurfs (film)|Smurfs]]'' (2025).

Rihanna is the recipient of [[List of awards and nominations received by Rihanna|numerous accolades]], including nine [[Grammy Award]]s, twelve [[Billboard Music Award|''Billboard'' Music Awards]], thirteen [[American Music Award]]s, and seven [[MTV Video Music Awards]]. She is the highest-[[RIAA certification|certified]] female digital single artist by the [[Recording Industry Association of America]], and has eight diamond-certified singles and [[List of Billboard Hot 100 chart achievements and milestones#Most number-one songs|fourteen number-one singles]] on the ''Billboard'' Hot 100. The [[List of music artists by net worth|first female musician to achieve billionaire status]], Rihanna founded the nonprofit [[Clara Lionel Foundation]], the cosmetics brand [[Fenty Beauty]], and the fashion brand [[Fenty (fashion house)|Fenty]] under [[LVMH]]. She was named an ambassador by the [[Government of Barbados]] in 2018 and declared a [[National Hero of Barbados]] in 2021.
